name: The Premium Finance Gap Calculator
problem: Losing policy renewals because clients cannot afford the 20-30% premium down payment. 1, 2
coreLogic: Calculates (Total Premium * Down Payment %) - Available Cash to find the exact financing gap. 1

name: The Urgent Tax Advance Estimator
problem: Clients owe IRS, risk penalties, and lack the liquid cash to pay. 3, 4
coreLogic: Advance = Avg Rev * 0.5 to 1.5; Penalty Savings = Est. Tax * 0.005 * Months Past Due. 3

name: The Gig Income Funding Predictor ("Giggle Finance")
problem: Need instant funding predictions based on app revenue without hard credit pulls. 5, 6
outputs: Back on the Road Funding Score 5
coreLogic: Avg Weekly Earnings * 4, modified by a seniority factor (e.g., *1.2 for 2+ years). 5

name: The NECA Material Money Quoter
problem: Winning a contract but lacking the upfront cash flow to buy materials before the first draw. 7, 8
coreLogic: Advance = Estimated Material Cost (capped at 25% of total job value or $50k). 7

name: The "Turndown Retrieval" Script Generator
problem: Bank officers are throwing away rejected leads and losing out on commissions. 9, 10
coreLogic: Inserts agent and bank branch names into a dynamic "50/50 commission split" pitch script. 9

name: The Blue Collar Banker Lead Qualifier
problem: Agents wasting time pitching non-fundable leads. 11
coreLogic: Deducts points from 100% for each missed underwriting minimum (Revenue, FICO, Time in Business). 11

name: The Daily Commission Projection Dashboard
problem: Need immediate, motivational calculations of daily earning potential. 12
coreLogic: Target Goal / Avg Commission = Deals Needed; Deals * 5 = Required Weekly Leads. 12

name: The Auto-Repair Emergency Advance Tool
problem: Car breaks down, and no cash for repairs means no ability to earn income. 13, 14
coreLogic: Funds up to 100% of the repair estimate, capped at $5,000 based on ride history. 13

name: The Merchant Cash Advance (MCA) Repayment Estimator
problem: Uncertainty about how daily MCA deductions will impact operating cash flow. 15
coreLogic: Total Repayment = Advance * Factor Rate; Daily Payment = Total Repayment / 90. 15

name: The Competitor "Meet or Beat" Submitter
problem: Skepticism about rates and predatory terms from other lenders. 16, 17
coreLogic: Captures competitor offer and verifies qualification for the $500 "Beat It" guarantee. 16

name: The Referral Commission Payout Estimator
problem: Affiliates want transparent expectations on exact commission payouts. 18
coreLogic: Client Funding Amount * Base Rate (Adjusted by New/Bronze/Silver tier). 18

name: The Buy-Here-Pay-Here Down Payment Bridge
problem: Customer is short on a vehicle down payment, killing the car sale. 19
coreLogic: Vehicle Down Payment Required - Customer Cash Available (Capped at $1,500). 19

name: The Trade Association Profit-Split Calculator
problem: Associations need non-dues revenue streams. 20, 21
coreLogic: Members * 5% Funding Rate * Avg Amount * 1% Override = Annual Revenue. 20

name: The Credit Score vs. Cash Flow Analyzer
problem: Feeling penalized by traditional banks for low FICO scores despite strong revenue. 22
coreLogic: Compares FICO (<600 = Bank Fail) against Monthly Deposits (>$5k = Breezy Pass). 22

name: The Cold Lead Monetization Calculator
problem: Throwing away rejected lease applications instead of monetizing them. 23
coreLogic: Rejected Leads * 15% Conversion * Avg Amount * 3% Comm * Split. 23

name: The Payroll Bridge Funding Quoter
problem: Client Net-30 payments are delayed, and Friday payroll must be met. 24
coreLogic: Recommends an advance equal to Total Weekly Payroll * 2 (up to $100k). 24

name: The Trucker Repair-to-Revenue Tracker
problem: Expensive truck repairs halt cash flow, causing panic. 25
coreLogic: Calculates Days to Recover = Repair Cost / (Avg Weekly Revenue / 7). 25

name: The Passive Income Network Builder
problem: Needs to see the exponential power of recruiting sub-agents to build downlines. 26
coreLogic: (Recruits * 4) * Avg T1 Commission * 0.5% override = Tier 2/3 Est. 26

name: The "Bank Firing" Risk Score
problem: Risk of rejection due to tightening Tier-1 banking standards. 27
coreLogic: Deducts points from 100 for <$250k Rev or <700 FICO. Score <50 = 85% Rejection Risk. 27

name: The Equipment Leasing Deposit Calculator
problem: Customers cannot afford the initial large security deposit for equipment leases. 28
coreLogic: Advance = Required Lease Deposit/Payment (capped at $25,000). 28

name: The Same-Day vs. 60-Day Cost Analyzer
problem: Waiting 60 days for a cheap bank loan costs more in lost revenue than a fast, expensive loan. 29
coreLogic: Average Daily Revenue * Days Lost Waiting for Bank = Revenue Lost. 29

name: The Tax Prep Fee Advance Solution
problem: Clients need cash for their IRS tax bill and the preparer's fee. 30
coreLogic: Advances the Tax Preparation Fee (up to $2,500) directly to the preparer's account. 30

name: The "Bad Credit, Good Business" Certifier
problem: Psychological discouragement from prior rejections. 31
coreLogic: If Months of Positive Cash Flow > 6, it overrides FICO and outputs a "Pass". 31

name: The Service Industry Tip-to-Revenue Translator
problem: Need to quantify credit card tips to prove steady revenue capacity to underwriters. 32
coreLogic: Repayment Capacity = (Daily CC Tip Rev + Daily CC Sale Rev) * 0.15. 32

name: The NECA Endorsement Value Illustrator
problem: Skepticism regarding alternative lender trust. 33
coreLogic: Outputs a high trust factor based on national trade association (NECA) backing. 33

name: The Competitor Rate-to-Factor Rate Translator
problem: Confusion comparing traditional time-based APRs to fixed MCA Factor Rates. 34
coreLogic: Translates Factor Rate (e.g., 1.18) to "$1.18 paid for every $1.00 borrowed". 34

name: Cash Flow Gap Bridge Calculator
problem: Finding the minimum MCA needed to cover immediate payroll/invoice gaps in under 60 seconds. 35
coreLogic: Cash Flow Gap Amount * 1.25 (Factor Buffer) = Needed MCA. 35

name: Fix-and-Flip ROI & Max Loan Estimator
problem: Hard money deal analysis to find max loan amount and potential ROI. 36
coreLogic: Max Loan = ARV * 0.75 LTV (Aligns with Hard Money limits). 36

name: Startup Funding Eligibility Quiz
problem: Brand new businesses (0 months) need pre-qualification without business history. 37
coreLogic: If FICO > 700 & Tradelines >= 5 -> Green Light (Startup Loan). Else -> Credit Incubator. 37

name: "Bank-Rejected" Second Look Finder
problem: Identifying viable alternatives after a rigid traditional bank denial. 38
coreLogic: Maps bank rejection reasons (e.g., Tax Lien, Time in Biz) to GoKapital's lenient products. 38

name: Real Estate Proof of Funds (POF) Generator
problem: Need an instant Proof of Funds letter to submit aggressive real estate offers. 39
coreLogic: Generates a conditional pre-qualification up to 80% LTV of the input offer amount. 39

name: SBA Denial Pivot Tool
problem: Cannot wait the 1-3 months required for SBA 7(a) loan approval. 40
coreLogic: If time <30 days, pivots to MCA/Term. If >90 days, anchors to SBA with a Term alternative. 40

name: Bad Credit Business Funding Match
problem: Need to match a sub-prime profile to the lowest-barrier GoKapital product. 41
coreLogic: Maps FICO: None->MCA, 500->Term Loan, 580->Equipment, 650->Hard Money. 41

name: Equipment Payment Estimator
problem: Need to estimate monthly costs for financing business equipment with bad credit. 42
coreLogic: Applies 6% to 35% cost range based on low-FICO (580+) equipment financing rates. 42

name: Lender-Readiness Score
problem: Assessing profiles against full criteria to create a 60-day action plan for unbankable leads. 43
coreLogic: Assigns 0-100 score and specific gap tasks (e.g., "Get 2 More Tradelines"). 43

name: Competitor Rejection Analyzer
problem: Rejected by algorithm-only competitors (e.g., OnDeck, BlueVine). 44
coreLogic: Maps competitor's rigid algorithm constraints to GoKapital's flexible human brokerage. 44

name: Invoice-to-Cash Flow Gap Analyzer
problem: Need to calculate exact cash runway before a late client payment destroys them. 45
coreLogic: Cash Runway = (Cash on Hand / Monthly Expenses) * 30. Triggers alert if Runway < Payment Days. 45

name: Gig Worker "Back on the Road" Calculator
problem: Vehicle is in the shop; daily lost income exceeds the cost of financing the repair. 46
coreLogic: Lost Income = Daily Earnings * Days Out. Compares to Repair Cost to justify emergency funding. 46

name: Tax Payment Advance Estimator
problem: Need quarterly tax funding to avoid IRS installment plan penalties. 47
outputs: IRS Payment Plan vs. Advance Comparison 47
coreLogic: Estimated Tax Owed = (Gross - Expenses) * Est. Tax Rate. Compares loan cost vs IRS penalty. 47

name: Material Cost Inflation Tracker
problem: Material prices surged since the bid; need to cover the budget gap. 48
coreLogic: Applies indexed inflation rates (e.g., copper +12%) to original budget to display "Funding Gap". 48

name: Premium Finance "Double Commission" Calc
problem: Agents losing renewals to cash flow want to see how funding clients makes them more money. 49
coreLogic: (Clients Lost * Premium * Comm Rate) + Funding Commission = New Revenue. 49

name: "Meet or Beat" Rate Comparison Tool
problem: Want to verify GoKapital's $500 guarantee against a current MCA offer. 50
coreLogic: Standardizes input factor rates/APRs. If GoKapital beats by 5%+, qualifies for guarantee. 50

name: Equipment ROI & Payment Calculator
problem: Checking if new equipment revenue outpaces the monthly financing payment. 51
coreLogic: If Expected Additional Monthly Revenue > Estimated Payment + 20%, outputs Green Light. 51

name: Restaurant "Walk-In Emergency" Tool
problem: Critical kitchen equipment (walk-in fridge) fails, causing daily spoilage/lost sales. 52
coreLogic: Urgency Score = Daily Revenue Lost * 7 days. If > Replacement Cost, triggers emergency path. 52

name: Payroll Gap Bridge Estimator
problem: Need exact funding required to hit Friday payroll while waiting on receivables. 53
coreLogic: Total Payroll Amount - Current Cash Available = Funding Needed. Suggests Term/MCA. 53

name: Embedded Finance Widget
problem: Partners need a white-label pre-qualification tool embedded on their own websites. 54
coreLogic: Collects Revenue/Need/Contact and routes directly to GoKapital CRM with partner tracking. 54

name: Wholesaler/Real Estate "Deal Analyzer" Pro
problem: Needs to run full deal math AND generate a proof of funds instantly. 55
coreLogic: (ARV * 0.75) - Rehab. If margin >15%, auto-generates conditional POF letter for property. 55

name: "Turndown Retrieval" Lead Magnet Template
problem: Need a ready-made asset to hand to rejected applicants to salvage the lead. 56
coreLogic: Pre-written explanations of bank denials combined with partner's custom referral link. 56

name: "Daily Pay Commission" Calculator
problem: Want to compare GoKapital's daily payouts against traditional Net-30 affiliate programs. 57
coreLogic: (Weekly Deals * Deal Size * Commission) / 5 days = Daily Income. 57

name: Supply House Partnership Landing Page
problem: Agents need a digital asset to pitch supply houses on offering contractor financing. 58
coreLogic: Pre-built "Help Your Customers Buy More" HTML form to capture supply house contacts. 58

name: "Credit Incubator" Roadmap Generator
problem: Needs a 60-90 day rehabilitation plan after being rejected for all standard funding. 59
outputs: Your Path to Funding Approval PDF 59
coreLogic: Identifies specific gaps (e.g., need 3 more months in biz, 2 more tradelines) and builds step plan. 59

name: The "Stockout Death Spiral" Estimator
problem: Underestimating the true cost of stocking out (lost sales + lost SEO ranking). 60
coreLogic: (Daily Rev * Days Out) + Estimated Re-Ranking Ad Spend + CLV Loss. 60

name: The Equity-Saver Shield (VC vs. 8fig)
problem: Thinking VC money is "free" without realizing the millions lost in equity dilution at exit. 61
coreLogic: (Exit Value * Equity %) - (8fig Cost of Capital) = Total Savings. 61

name: The "Boat-to-Bank" Gap Analyzer
problem: Cash is "dead" and inaccessible while inventory is trapped on cargo ships. 62
coreLogic: Maps the "Dead Zone" days between factory "Cash Out" and Amazon "First Dollar In". 62

name: The MCA "Shark" Auditor
problem: Merchant Cash Advances hide their triple-digit APRs behind opaque "Factor Rates". 63
coreLogic: Converts Factor Rate and Daily Holdback % into an effective annualized APR. 63

name: The "Viral Hit" Stress Test
problem: A viral video spikes demand 5x, leading to an immediate stockout and brand death. 64
coreLogic: Stock / (Monthly Units * 5). Fails stress test if result < Supplier Lead Time. 64

name: The "Asset-Light" ROI Multiplier
problem: Unsure whether to allocate capital to inventory replenishment or Facebook Ads. 65
coreLogic: Compares the compounding ROI of flipping inventory against one-time ad attribution. 65

name: The "Amazon Payout" Bridge Predictor
problem: Amazon's 14-day payout hold kills the ability to pay the next manufacturing deposit. 66
coreLogic: Calculates the gap between Amazon's disbursement schedule and the factory's PO due date. 66

name: The Q4 "War-Room" PO Planner
problem: Ordering inventory too late for major events (Black Friday, Chinese New Year). 67
coreLogic: Back-calculates the exact date a PO must be funded to land 14 days before the sales event. 67

name: The "Ghost Revenue" Interceptor
problem: Blindness to the massive invisible revenue lost from historical stockouts. 68
coreLogic: Days Out of Stock * Avg Daily Sales = Ghost Revenue. 68

name: The "Landed Cost" Profit Leak Finder
problem: Ignoring hidden logistics costs (storage, shipping spikes) resulting in false profit views. 68
coreLogic: Revenue - (Factory Price + Shipping + Storage + Ad CPA) = Net Net Margin. 68

name: The "Exit Value" Accelerator
problem: Business valuation/EBITDA is suppressed because of historical stockout periods. 69
coreLogic: (New EBITDA * Multiple) - Current Valuation = Valuation Increase. 69

name: The "Air Freight" Panic Button
problem: Deciding if 5x air freight costs are worth it to avoid a 45-day sea freight stockout. 70
coreLogic: If (Profit * Days Saved) > (Air Cost - Sea Cost), recommendation is to fly it. 70

name: The "Big Box" Trap Simulator (Net-90)
problem: Landing a huge retail PO but going bankrupt fulfilling it due to Net-90 payment terms. 71
coreLogic: Plots Cash Balance over 90 days. If the line crosses zero, seller dies before getting paid. 71

name: The "Zombie SKU" Hunter
problem: 80% of operating cash is tied up in 20% of dead inventory that refuses to sell. 72
coreLogic: Marks SKU as "Zombie" if supply >6 months and margin contribution <10%. 72

name: The "Ad Cost" Inflation Forecaster
problem: Budgeting based on summer CPA, leading to cash burnout when Q4 CPMs spike. 73
coreLogic: Applies a "Q4 Multiplier" (e.g., 1.4x) to ad costs, showing when profit turns to loss. 73

name: The "Golden Quarter" Bonus Calculator
problem: Founders pay themselves last and want to visualize their personal take-home pay potential. 74
coreLogic: Revenue * Net Margin * (1 - Reinvest %) = Personal Founder Bonus. 74

name: The "Supplier Bully" Power Meter
problem: Overseas factories dictate harsh terms (30% deposit upfront) because the seller is small. 75
coreLogic: Compares volume to benchmarks. If >$500k, seller has leverage to demand Net-30 terms. 75

name: The "Discount" Death Spiral Check
problem: Running deep discount sales for cash flow, unknowingly destroying unit economics. 76
coreLogic: Standard break-even analysis calculating the massive volume increase needed to maintain profit. 76

name: The Amazon "Fees & Fines" Auditor
problem: FBA storage and low-inventory fees silently eating margins before payouts hit. 77
coreLogic: Applies current Amazon rate cards to dimensions/storage days to reveal real FBA take-rate. 77

name: The "Subscription Box" Churn Burner
problem: Ordering physical goods before renewals process; high churn results in dead stock. 78
coreLogic: (Subscribers * (1 - Churn)) * Cost. Calculates cash wasted against churn volatility. 78

name: The "Influencer" Break-Even Calculator
problem: Paying influencers upfront without knowing the actual product volume needed to break even. 78
coreLogic: Fee / Margin = Units to Break Even; Units / Conv Rate = Traffic Needed. 78

name: The "3PL" Switching Cost Calculator
problem: Hating current 3PL but paralyzed by the massive one-time cost of moving pallets of stock. 79
coreLogic: Move Cost / ((Current Fee - New Fee) * Monthly Vol) = Months to Break Even. 79

name: The DD+7 Impact Calculator
problem: Amazon's DD+7 policy change permanently locks up a massive portion of working capital. 80
coreLogic: (New DD+7 Cycle - Current Payout Cycle) * Average Daily Sales = Cash Loss. 80

name: The Inventory Turnover Accelerator
problem: Reluctance to pay a 2% advance fee because they don't see the velocity ROI. 81
coreLogic: Calculates Turns Lost waiting 14 days, then applies a 30% velocity multiplier for daily payouts. 81

name: The "Fee Nullifier" Cash Back Calculator
problem: Objection to advance fees masking the offsetting value of 2% cash back cards. 82
coreLogic: Gross Fee (Advance * 2%) - Cash Back (Advance * Reinvest % * 2%) = Net Cost. 82

name: TikTok Shop Viral Curse Predictor
problem: TikTok holds funds for 15-30 days; viral sales cause stockouts and late dispatch suspensions. 83
coreLogic: Current Cash / (Units Sold * Supplier Cost). If < 1.0, triggers "Critical" suspension risk. 83

name: Account Level Reserve Hostage Calculator
problem: Visceral anger at earned money being held hostage in the abstract "Account Level Reserve". 84
coreLogic: Reserve Balance * Avg Net Profit Margin * 1 extra turn/mo = Missed Profit. 84

name: The CPA/Bookkeeper Fee Integration Tool
problem: CPAs hate chasing clients for invoices who are waiting on Amazon payouts. 85
coreLogic: Uses the Average Client Invoicing Delay to output an integration checklist to guarantee CPA fees. 85

name: The Supply Chain Hostage Release Timer
problem: Missing a supplier wire deadline causes a catastrophic Q4 stockout. 86
coreLogic: Out of Stock Units * (Days Since OOS * Avg Daily Unit Sales) = Missed Sales Revenue. 86

name: The FBA Prep Center Payout Predictor
problem: Prep centers suffering cash flow delays because their seller clients are cash-poor. 87
coreLogic: Guarantees prep center invoice payment within 24 hours of Payability funding the seller. 87

name: The "Black Box" Amazon Lending Score
problem: Amazon Lending is a black box, invite-only, and vanishes when sales dip. 88
coreLogic: If no active invite, status is "LOCKED", positioning Payability as the immediate alternative. 88

name: The Cost of Waiting vs. Cost of Fee Analyzer
problem: Rationalizing the cost of borrowing capital against the opportunity cost of waiting. 89
coreLogic: Compares Margin vs 2% Fee. If Margin > Fee, nets a win by allowing 14 extra days of sales. 89

name: The Wholesale Distributor Payout Gap Closer
problem: Distributors forced to buy less profitable items because their cash is constrained. 90
coreLogic: Total Purchase Volume * (Desired Stock Margin - Constrained Stock Margin) = Profit Gap. 90

name: The Multi-Channel Expansion Forecaster
problem: Missing revenue by not expanding to Walmart/Shopify due to lack of setup capital. 91
coreLogic: Amazon Monthly Rev * 12 * Assumed Lift % = Missed Annual Revenue from not expanding. 91

name: The Seasonal Stock-Up Stress Reliever
problem: Panic about not ordering enough holiday inventory fast enough due to lead times. 92
coreLogic: (Daily Sales * Peak Multiplier * Lead Time) - Current Stock mapped to a funding timeline. 92

name: The Freight Forwarder Funding Guarantee
problem: Forwarders facing massive payment risk on shipping/duties invoices from cash-poor sellers. 93
coreLogic: Assures forwarder that client cash is released 14-28 days faster, eliminating invoice risk. 93

name: The Q4 Profit Erosion Forecaster
problem: Taking high-interest emergency MCAs during peak season erodes all net profit. 94
coreLogic: Total Q4 Inventory Needed * Interest Rate of Alternative Loan = Avoided Interest Cost. 94

name: The Customer Review Velocity Builder
problem: Slow cash limits launch inventory, delaying customer reviews and organic ranking. 95
coreLogic: Days Saved * Avg Daily Sales * Review Conversion Rate = Extra Reviews Gained. 95

name: The "Bezos Interest" Calculator
problem: Visualizes the opportunity cost of Amazon holding seller funds for 14 days. 96
coreLogic: Payout * (ROI% / 100) * (26 periods / 12). Calculates profit generated if reinvested instantly. 97

name: The Stockout "Bleed" Auditor
problem: Quantifies the dollar damage of waiting for a wire transfer while out of stock. 98
coreLogic: (Daily Sales * Profit * Days) + a 1.5x penalty for lost BSR ranking recovery costs. 98

name: The "Velocity Multiplier" Simulator
problem: Need to see the exponential math of flipping inventory 4x a month instead of 1x. 99
coreLogic: Models compound capital growth over 90 days at 30-day (standard) vs 7-day (OnRamp) intervals. 99

name: The "DD+7" Reality Calendar
problem: Confusion around Amazon's shift to a "Delivery Date + 7 Days" reserve policy. 100
coreLogic: Amazon Date (Ship + Delivery + 7 + 3) vs OnRamp Date (Ship + 0). Highlights the Danger Zone. 100

name: The Prep Center "Shock" Audit (2026)
problem: Amazon ending FBA prep means 3PLs demand upfront cash, gridlocking supply chains. 101
coreLogic: Monthly Units * Prep Fee = Upfront Cash Requirement needed before Amazon pays out. 101

name: The Wholesale "Deal Sniper" Analyzer
problem: Staring at a limited-time 40% off clearance lot and needing a go/no-go funding decision. 102
coreLogic: If (Projected Profit - Advance Fee) / Deal Cost > 10%, outputs "SNIPE IT". 102

name: The "Unbankable" Fundability Quiz
problem: Rejected by DTC-brand-only lenders (like Wayflyer) because they sell arbitrage goods. 103
coreLogic: If Rev > $3k and Platform = Amazon, issues instant approval based on store data, not FICO. 103

name: The Q4 "Load Up" Capacity Calculator
problem: Limited cash limits the volume of inventory a seller can front-load for Q4. 104
coreLogic: Simulates 3-4 funding/restock cycles within the short Q4 window to show maximum capacity. 104

name: The "Deferred Transaction" Unlocker
problem: Anger over Amazon labeling earned cash as "Deferred Transactions" in Seller Central. 105
coreLogic: Dashboard Balance * 0.80 = Instantly Unlockable Amount within 4 hours. 105

name: The "Zombie Inventory" Detector
problem: Aged inventory storage surcharges eating away at margins. 106
coreLogic: Compares long-term holding cost against liquidating and reinvesting via OnRamp. 106

name: The "Payroll Panic" Countdown Timer
problem: Waiting on Net-60 GC check while Friday payroll approaches, risking crew walk-off. 107, 108
coreLogic: Current Balance / (Payroll * 1.18 / 5) = Days to Zero. Alerts if < Payment Date. 108

name: The "Bid Protector" Inflation Adjuster
problem: Bidding jobs 3 months out while volatile material prices (copper/PVC) eat margins. 109
coreLogic: Material Cost * (Inflation Factor * Duration). Outputs a Risk Buffer to add to the bid. 109

name: The "Net-90" Opportunity Cost Calculator
problem: Thinking saving a 2% funding fee is better than getting cash early to bid new jobs. 110
coreLogic: Annualized Cost of Waiting = (Potential Jobs Missed * Avg Profit) / Invoice Amount. 110

name: The "Repair vs. Replace" ROI Engine
problem: Semi-truck engine blows; emotional decision between a $25k rebuild or an $80k new truck. 111
coreLogic: Calculates Cash Flow Difference between the Repair Path and Replace Path over 12 months. 111

name: The "Spot Market" Breakeven Calculator
problem: Spot rates drop, truckers unsure of minimum Rate Per Mile (RPM) to cover insurance/fuel. 112
coreLogic: Fixed CPM + Variable CPM + (Desired Profit / Miles) = Minimum Acceptable Target Rate. 112

name: The "Deal Killer" Hard Money Analyzer
problem: Need to know instantly if 12% hard money interest kills a distressed property deal. 113
coreLogic: Profit = ARV - (Loan + Interest + 3% Closing). If Profit > 15% ARV, Green Light. 113

name: The "Am I Bankable?" Probability Score
problem: Terrified of rejection and hard credit inquiries ruining their score. 114
coreLogic: Assigns 0-100 score filtering for Revenue >$15k, FICO >600, Time >1Yr. Routes to specific tiers. 114

name: The Section 179 "Tax Slash" Calculator
problem: Hesitating on Q4 equipment purchases to reduce taxable income. 115
coreLogic: Equipment Cost * Tax Bracket (assuming 100% depreciation) = Tax Savings / Net Cost. 115

name: The "Burn Rate" Runway Extender
problem: Burning cash fast and needing to know the exact "Death Date" before missing payroll. 116
coreLogic: Current Cash / (Monthly Expenses - Monthly Revenue) = Months of Runway Left. 116

name: The "Bad Debt" Consolidator
problem: Crushed by daily deductions from multiple existing Merchant Cash Advances. 117
coreLogic: Compares current daily burn (Payments * 22) against a new 24-month consolidation term. 117

name: The "Equity Defender" Simulator
problem: Ignorance to the pain of venture capital dilution until Series C ruins their exit payout. 118
coreLogic: Visualizes a side-by-side growth curve of VC equity bleed vs. a 0% equity-loss Moonshine route. 118

name: The "Bad Debt" Detector (MCA Killer)
problem: Predatory MCA lenders disguise 100%+ APRs as simple "factor rates" in PDFs. 119
coreLogic: OCR scans uploaded PDF offer, calculates the true APR, and flags "DANGER" or "SAFE". 119

name: The "Runway Extender" Dashboard
problem: Fear of hitting zero cash; requires visual proof of how non-dilutive capital saves them. 120
coreLogic: Connects to Plaid, analyzes burn rate, and visually pushes the "Death Date" out 6-12 months. 120

name: The "Deal Memo" Generator (AI Agent)
problem: Hates writing extensive funding applications or executive summaries from scratch. 121
coreLogic: Conversational AI asks 5-6 questions and formats answers into a banker-ready summary. 121

name: The "Recession-Proof" Stress Tester
problem: Unaware of how vulnerable their cash flow is to macroeconomic shocks. 122
coreLogic: AI scrapes user's URL and simulates 3 market crash scenarios tailored to their specific industry. 122

name: The "Compliance & KYC" Vault
problem: Deal fatigue caused by messy corporate documents (EINs, Articles, Voided Checks). 123
coreLogic: Users drop messy docs; system auto-verifies, renames, and packages them cleanly. 123

name: The "Investor Data Room" Template
problem: Need a professional, secure data room to send financials to investors instantly. 124
coreLogic: Generates a cloneable, password-protected Next.js template hosted on Vercel. 124

name: The "Link-in-Bio" Finance Deck
problem: Standard PDF pitch decks look terrible and are unreadable on mobile devices. 125
coreLogic: Form-to-site generator that builds a scrollable, mobile-optimized pitch page with a lock gate. 125

name: The "Fundability Score" Widget
problem: B2B partners need a viral tool on their own site to capture leads for Moonshine. 125
coreLogic: User inputs monthly revenue; widget generates a speedometer showing instant accessible capital. 125

name: The "Cost of Capital" Comparator
problem: Demands radical transparency when comparing Moonshine vs VC vs MCA. 126
coreLogic: Interactive table highlighting total payback amount and total equity lost across three routes. 126

name: The "Funding Simulator"
problem: Needs a gamified way to visualize avoiding bankruptcy by adding credit lines. 127, 128
coreLogic: Renders a "Runway Chart" crashing to zero; user toggles a $50k LOC to extend the green line. 128

name: The "Cash Command Center" Dashboard
problem: Unaware of daily financial leaks, phantom subscriptions, and cash bleed. 129
coreLogic: Plaid-connected n8n AI agent analyzes 30-day history and sends a daily "Savage Wake-Up Call". 129

name: The "Pitch Deck Roaster"
problem: Terrified their pitch deck is boring or lacking critical financial metrics like CAC. 130
coreLogic: OpenAI Vision scans slides as a "Wall Street Wolf" persona, giving specific, ruthless feedback. 130

name: The "Amazon DD+7 Survival Calculator"
problem: Amazon's new DD+7 policy creates a 17-21 day cash flow gap that kills restocking. 131, 132
coreLogic: Analyzes Monthly Sales + Avg Shipping Time to output the exact capital Amazon holds hostage. 132

name: The "Bankability Scorecard"
problem: Banks auto-reject up to 92% of applications; users need an alternative "Einstein Predictor". 131, 132
coreLogic: 6-question quiz calculates a surrogate FICO-SBSS score to bypass traditional gatekeepers. 132

name: The "Mentorship ROI Accelerator"
problem: Coaches lose 30% of $10k mastermind sales because students lack upfront liquidity. 131, 132
coreLogic: Shows how 0% credit stacking lets students pay $200/mo while using $10k for ad-spend fuel. 132

name: 70-Second Eligibility Score
problem: Instantly filters users into Startup (0% interest) vs Revenue-Based paths.
coreLogic: nan

name: Investor Data Room Template (Vercel Deploy)
audience: Founders raising capital; acquisition entrepreneurs; indie SaaS builders
problem: Founders need a clean, investor-ready place to host the deck, financials, and team info without emailing messy attachments.
outputs: Password-protected data room link + upload structure
coreLogic: Every investor touchpoint is a trust test. This makes you look organized in minutes.

name: Fundability Score Widget
audience: SMB owners; ecommerce sellers; gig/self-employed; agencies screening clients
problem: People guess at fundability. This gives an instant ‘where you stand’ snapshot and next steps.
outputs: Fundability score band + best-fit product paths
coreLogic: Turns vague interest into a concrete number—and a clear next action.

name: Cash Flow Gap Bridge Calculator
problem: Calculates the exact MCA amount needed to cover payroll while waiting for a Net-30 check.
coreLogic: nan

name: Amazon Stockout Death Spiral
problem: Quantifies revenue lost per hour during an inventory stockout vs cost of capital.
coreLogic: nan

name: Cost of Capital Comparator
audience: Founders comparing offers; brokers; finance ops leads
problem: Most offers hide the real cost. This makes payback and trade-offs obvious (MCA vs LOC vs equity).
coreLogic: Transparency as a weapon—prevents expensive ‘fast money’ mistakes.

name: Equity Defender Simulator
audience: Startups & SaaS founders considering VC vs non-dilutive capital
problem: Visualizes how dilution compounds across rounds vs non-dilutive paths.
coreLogic: People don’t ‘feel’ dilution until they see it. This makes the pain visible.

name: Bad Debt Detector (MCA Killer)
audience: SMBs offered MCA/fast funding; founders under time pressure
problem: Extracts terms from offers and calculates true APR + red flags (factor-rate traps).
outputs: True cost report + red-flag list + safer alternatives
coreLogic: Saves you from signing the financial equivalent of a bear trap.

name: Runway Extender Dashboard
audience: Startups; agencies; ecommerce brands watching cash
problem: Projects burn + ‘death date’ and shows how different capital injections extend runway.
coreLogic: Urgency becomes math. Math becomes action.

name: Deal Memo Generator (AI Agent)
audience: Founders applying for capital; acquisition buyers; brokers prepping submissions
problem: Turns messy founder answers into a lender/investor-ready executive summary / deal memo.
outputs: Banker-style deal memo PDF + submission-ready summary
coreLogic: Professional packaging increases approvals and speed.

name: Recession-Proof Stress Tester
audience: SMBs; ecommerce; agencies; local service businesses
problem: Simulates downturn scenarios to reveal cashflow vulnerabilities and buffers (LOC/working capital).
outputs: Risk report + mitigation actions + capital buffer plan
coreLogic: Better to discover weakness in a dashboard than in bankruptcy court.

name: Compliance & KYC Vault
audience: Founders who hate paperwork; SMB owners; brokers collecting docs
problem: Organizes, verifies, and packages business docs into a funding-ready bundle.
outputs: Verified doc set + standardized naming + ZIP export
coreLogic: Cuts underwriting friction and prevents ‘missing-doc’ delays.

name: Link-in-Bio Finance Deck (Pitch Page Generator)
audience: Founders raising capital on LinkedIn/Twitter; creators; indie SaaS
problem: Creates a mobile-first ‘pitch page’ so founders can share one clean link (not a PDF).
outputs: Scrollable pitch page link + gated downloads + booking button
coreLogic: Modern fundraising happens on phones. PDFs die there.

name: Funding Simulator (Capital Stack Playground)
audience: Founders deciding between LOC/RBF/advance; finance ops leads
problem: Lets users toggle funding options and see runway/cashflow impact like a finance sandbox.
outputs: Runway + cashflow chart with option toggles
coreLogic: Turns ‘maybe’ into numbers—and numbers into decisions.

name: Cash Command Center (SaaS-lite Dashboard)
audience: Operators; SMB owners; agencies; ecommerce founders
problem: A persistent dashboard that surfaces cash signals + daily ‘wake-up call’ insights.
outputs: Daily pulse insights + cash alerts + action list
coreLogic: Creates a habit. When you need funding, the button is already there.

name: Pitch Deck Roaster (AI Roast Report)
audience: Founders; acquisition entrepreneurs; anyone pitching investors/lenders
problem: Gives ruthless, specific feedback on decks and outputs a ‘roast report’ + fundability score.
coreLogic: A good deck gets meetings. A bad deck gets ignored.
